confidence_rationale: Video is a SAGES 2015 Surgical Spring Week presentation on 'Validity Evidence for Novel Advanced Laparoscopic Suturing Tasks' by Yusuke Watanabe, MD and colleagues. This is an educational/research presentation with slides, no actual task performance video. Content includes research objectives, advanced suturing models (Needle Handling, Under Tension, Continuous Suturing), study methods, participant data (18 senior residents, 13 MIS surgeons), performance comparisons, validity correlations, and conclusions. No trainee performing an FLS task is visible.

demonstrated_techniques: Advanced suturing model concepts: Needle Handling, Under Tension, Continuous Suturing
Presentation of validity evidence framework for measuring laparoscopic suturing skills
Research-based comparison of resident vs. MIS surgeon performance metrics
verbal_cues: 60% of program graduates felt NOT proficient in laparoscopic suturing (Matter et al., Annals of Surgery 2014)
Objective: Develop advanced suturing models and metrics; collect validity evidence
Performance correlates significantly with training level and self-reported experience
Spearman correlation coefficients (r) between 0.51–0.70 across three task metrics
common_errors_addressed: Lack of proficiency in laparoscopic suturing among surgical graduates highlighted as motivating problem
Study addresses need for improved skills assessment via novel, validated task models
